items.headline: Mongolia confirms third prime minister within a year amid economic alarm
items.body: Parliament approved former hip-hop artist Uchral Nyam-Osor to break legislative deadlock and calm fears over fuel dependence and commodity volatility. Rapid turnover reflects deepening factional struggles in ruling party as landlocked nation faces compounding external pressures from energy costs and mining revenue swings.

items.headline: Japan deploys first domestically-developed long-range missiles to southwest bases
items.body: Type-12 missiles with ranges exceeding earlier versions became operational at Camp Kengun in response to China's military expansion. Deployment marks shift from postwar defensive posture to counterstrike capability as Tokyo fortifies southwestern islands near Taiwan and disputed waters.

items.headline: Britain cuts Africa aid by more than half to fund rearmament
items.body: Foreign Office redirects support away from Sierra Leone, Malawi and poorest nations toward Ukraine and Middle East conflict zones. Health officials warn withdrawn funding for disease outbreaks, maternal care and child nutrition will have generational consequences across sub-Saharan Africa.

items.headline: Rohingya refugees face sharply reduced food assistance as multiple donors retreat
items.body: World Food Program implements tiered system dropping some aid to seven dollars monthly for hundreds of thousands in Bangladesh camps. Cuts compound after major donor reductions leave overcrowded settlements with diminishing resources amid ongoing displacement crisis.

North Korean hackers breach software update system targeting cryptocurrency access

meanwhile.text: New oral GLP-1 medication Foundayo receives FDA approval with fewer restrictions
Supreme Court strikes down Colorado conversion therapy ban as free speech violation
meanwhile.search: Supreme Court conversion therapy Colorado ruling 2026

es.headline: Police open investigation after anti-Muslim chants mar Spain friendly match against Egypt
es.body: Catalan police investigate Islamophobic chanting during pre-World Cup friendly in Barcelona as fans repeatedly shouted discriminatory slogans and booed Egyptian anthem. Football authorities and government condemned incident while star player criticized those chanting as ignorant and racist, exposing tensions between Spain's anti-discrimination pledges and fan behavior.

uk.headline: Government announces pivot toward EU partnership as relations with Trump deteriorate
uk.body: Britain will seek closer cooperation with European Union on security and economy amid Iran war fallout and US criticism. Shift marks strategic realignment away from transatlantic alliance toward continental partners after decade of post-Brexit distance.

uk.headline: Channel migrant patrol deal with France collapses into short-term extension
uk.body: Negotiations stalled over performance-linked funding demands leaving only two-month stopgap arrangement in place. Hours after talks failed two migrants died attempting crossing as enforcement rates hit lowest point since crisis began.

nl.headline: Port workers authorize nationwide strike over retirement and benefit cuts
nl.body: Dockworkers across Dutch ports voted unanimously to strike for at least five days against government social security reforms. Operations at Rotterdam and other seaports are expected to largely halt during walkouts.
